strategy_criteria_summary: USA optionable stocks over $20 with market cap above $2B, 3M average volume above 1M, beta above 1, price above the 50/200-day SMAs, within 20% of the 52-week high, and RSI 40-70. This keeps the structure-driven pool liquid and tradeable without over-filtering it to zero.
scoring_reasons: trend alignment was clearly supportive|market/sector environment was clearly supportive|price structure was clearly supportive|best contract was long_call 2026-05-15 25
decision_reasoning: MP has the strongest underlying thesis in the slate: +18.5% 5d, +33.4% 20d, +24.9% RS vs SPY, 1.44x volume, data confidence 98. However, every available MP contract carries rich IV (96–144%) and OTM strikes requiring a sizable additional move, which caps contract quality. The May 15 80 strike is the least bad expression but still leaves breakeven ~12% away with only 0.26 delta — not a Standard recommend. GME offers a cleaner ATM 0.52-delta contract with IV only 54% and tighter spreads, but its directional edge is weak (RS ~1%, 5d +2.6% lagging SPY). BROS and SYM have wide spreads and deep-OTM strikes that disqualify them. Net: best setup to monitor, not enter — lands in watchlist band.
